header.label: Stage 6 Phase 7 (FINAL) — Tickets G + I + FF + XX + devplatform history doc

scope.summary:
Phase 7 is the final Stage 6.7.5 execution phase. It closes four correctness/ reliability tickets and adds one new documentation artifact, then declares Stage 6.7.5 complete. Remaining open tickets after Phase 7 migrate to the standard Phase 5 post-operational backlog.
Five work items:
(G) Ticket G (id 315, priority 2): python.exe absolute-path verification. Guide-#10 pre-inspection found all 6 python-invoking scheduled-task XMLs already use C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.venv\Scripts\python.exe (absolute). 7 remaining XMLs invoke wscript.exe + powershell.exe only (no python.exe). PS1 scripts have no python invocations. EA-16 MUST independently verify this via Check 0d before documenting. If all absolute: document the canonical pattern in devplatform/docs/governance/fleet-hygiene.md. If any relative paths found: fix them in C1, then document.
(I) Ticket I (id 283, priority 2): .mcp.json gitignore + credential disposition. Both .mcp.json files are in template format (${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}) after Phase 4 Ticket JJ. BlarAI/.gitignore already has .mcp.json entry. devplatform/.gitignore does NOT have .mcp.json entry (confirmed by Guide-#10). Fix: add .mcp.json to devplatform/.gitignore. Document final disposition: template format + gitignored is the canonical pattern.
(FF) Ticket FF (id 296, priority 2): n1(l) wrapper defect disposition. Stage 4 Item 4.11a smoke test used direct invocation (python -m tools.vikunja_mcp.cli list-projects) instead of a wrapper script. Guide-#10 found no wrapper script in vikunja_mcp directory. EA-16 MUST independently search for any wrapper (see Check 0e). If found: evaluate and remove (direct module invocation supersedes it). If not found: document "wrapper absent; direct module invocation is the established canonical pattern" in vikunja_mcp README.md.
(XX) Ticket XX (id 328, priority 2): blarai_next_task_resolver.py alignment. Guide-#10 read the file. Constructor uses DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT from _project_context (correct cross-repo pattern). No ambiguous docs/active_tasks.yaml references found in docstrings. EA-16 MUST independently read the full file and confirm. If aligned: close with no code changes. If drifted: cosmetic docstring fix only.
(H) devplatform history doc (no Vikunja ticket — Guide-#10 initiative): Create devplatform/docs/PLATFORM_SEPARATION_HISTORY.md. A lightweight pointer doc for future-devplatform context: extraction date, what moved, key architectural decisions, and a pointer to the full narrative at BlarAI/docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md.
IMPORTANT — complete_task RESTORED: The user restarted Claude Code before spawning EA-16. The Vikunja MCP server now runs the Phase 6 fixed server.py. mcp__vikunja__complete_task is safe to use directly. The A33 Option B workaround is no longer required. EA-16 MUST verify this via Check 0f before calling complete_task for the first time.
scope.acceptance: 1. All 13 scheduled-task XMLs confirmed: python-invoking ones use absolute venv paths; verified by scan. Any relative path found is fixed. fleet-hygiene.md updated. 2. devplatform/.gitignore contains .mcp.json entry. 3. Both .mcp.json files confirmed template format. Disposition documented. 4. n1(l) wrapper disposition recorded: removed (if found) or absent (documented). vikunja_mcp README.md updated with canonical invocation pattern. 5. blarai_next_task_resolver.py alignment confirmed; no changes or cosmetic fix only. 6. devplatform/docs/PLATFORM_SEPARATION_HISTORY.md created. 7. STATUS.md E15 entry records Phase 7 complete, Stage 6.7.5 closed. 8. Tickets G (315), I (283), FF (296), XX (328) all done=True in Vikunja.

background.item:
Ticket G was raised at Stage 4 preflight (2026-04-28) as a reliability risk: scheduled-task XMLs might invoke python via PATH lookup, which breaks when the venv is reinstalled or the user profile PATH changes between interactive and S4U (service) contexts.
Guide-#10 XML scan (2026-05-08) found: credentials-rotation-reminder.xml — C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.venv\Scripts\python.exe ✓ daily-digest.xml — C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.venv\Scripts\python.exe ✓ dashboard-maintainer.xml — C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.venv\Scripts\python.exe ✓ gate-stale-cleaner.xml — C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.venv\Scripts\python.exe ✓ weekly-summary.xml — C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.venv\Scripts\python.exe ✓ welcome-back-poll.xml — C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.venv\Scripts\python.exe ✓ agents-cadence-monitor.xml — wscript.exe → powershell.exe (no python.exe) ✓ escalation-watchdog.xml — wscript.exe → powershell.exe (no python.exe) ✓ wake-ea_code.xml — wscript.exe → powershell.exe (no python.exe) ✓ wake-co_lead_architect.xml — wscript.exe → powershell.exe (no python.exe) ✓ wake-sdo.xml — wscript.exe → powershell.exe (no python.exe) ✓ sprint-auditor.xml — wscript.exe → powershell.exe (no python.exe) ✓ toast-watchdog.xml — wscript.exe → powershell.exe (no python.exe) ✓
EA-16 must independently confirm this via Check 0d before treating as verified. The documentation target is devplatform/docs/governance/fleet-hygiene.md (this file exists — confirmed by Guide-#10).

Ticket I tracked the .mcp.json credential disposition through Phases C, 4, and JJ. Current state (Guide-#10 verified): BlarAI/.mcp.json — VIKUNJA_PASS = "${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}" (template) ✓ devplatform/.mcp.json — VIKUNJA_PASS = "${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}" (template) ✓ BlarAI/.gitignore — contains .mcp.json entry (line 103) ✓ devplatform/.gitignore — does NOT contain .mcp.json entry ✗ — THIS IS THE FIX
Both PS1 scripts were refactored in Phase 4 Ticket II to read $env:VIKUNJA_* directly (no longer read from .mcp.json at all). So the template format in .mcp.json is safe — only MCP loaders read it, and they resolve ${env:VAR} against the process environment.
Final disposition to document: "Both .mcp.json files use ${env:VIKUNJA_PASS} template format. Both are gitignored. PS1 fleet scripts read credentials from $env:VIKUNJA_URL/USER/PASS directly (Phase 4 Ticket II). This is the canonical credential pattern for BlarAI/devplatform."

Item 4.11a (Stage 4 EA-8 smoke test) required verifying the Vikunja MCP CLI was accessible. The spec referenced a "wrapper script" (ack n1 sub-item (l)) that should wrap `python -m tools.vikunja_mcp.cli`. During execution, the wrapper was defective or absent; EA-8 used direct invocation instead (Option α). Ticket FF was raised to either fix or formally retire the wrapper.
Guide-#10 found no wrapper script in devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/ (no .sh, .bat, or .ps1 wrapper files in that directory). The vikunja_mcp/cli.py itself IS the canonical entry point via `python -m tools.vikunja_mcp.cli`.
EA-16 should search broadly (see Check 0e) before concluding. If no wrapper found: update devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/README.md to explicitly document the canonical invocation as Option α: `python -m tools.vikunja_mcp.cli`.

Ticket XX was created by Guide-#10 at Phase 5 close to track the blarai_next_task_resolver.py alignment check deferred by EA-14.
Guide-#10 reading (2026-05-08): - Constructor: `repo_root: Path | str = _project_context.DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT` ✓ (correct cross-repo reference — same pattern used in Phase 5 Ticket VV) - No module-level docstring references to `docs/active_tasks.yaml` - `continuation_template` uses relative path resolved against `self._repo_root` which is anchored to BlarAI root — correct - No ambiguous "repo root" phrasing found
EA-16 reads the full file independently. If alignment confirmed: close with no changes. If docstring drift found: one-line cosmetic fix only.

devplatform/docs/PLATFORM_SEPARATION_HISTORY.md does not yet exist. The devplatform/docs/governance/ directory exists (fleet-hygiene.md lives there). devplatform/docs/ itself exists — creating a new .md file at the root of docs/ is valid.
Purpose: future-devplatform context. When a developer sees an unusual PS1 pattern, python path, or cross-repo constant and wonders "why?", this doc provides the breadcrumb trail to BlarAI/docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md where the full narrative lives.
Content outline (EA-16 may expand naturally): 1. What happened: fleet tools extracted from BlarAI to devplatform (Stage 4, 2026-04-28). 2. Pointer: full documentation at BlarAI/docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md. 3. Key decisions (with brief rationale): - Env-var credential pattern ($env:VIKUNJA_URL/USER/PASS) — Phase 4 Ticket II - Cross-repo path constant (DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT from _project_context) — Phase 5 - Absolute python.exe paths in scheduled-task XMLs — Phase 7 Ticket G - .mcp.json template format + gitignored — Phase 7 Ticket I - Direct module invocation for vikunja_mcp CLI — Phase 7 Ticket FF - wake_launcher fail-open posture on missing credentials — ADR embedded in fleet-hygiene.md 4. Stage 6.7.5 hardening: 7 execution phases, 2026-05-07 to 2026-05-08. Declared complete at Phase 7 close.

comprehension_gate.instruction: Respond with a comprehension gate covering CG1-CG7 exactly. Stop after the gate. Wait for Guide APPROVED before any pre-flight or edit.

items.item: List all five work items (G, I, FF, XX, H) with their Vikunja id (or "no ticket" for H), the type of work (verify+doc / gitignore fix / search+disposition / read+verify / new doc), and the primary file(s) each touches. State what "FINAL" means for Phase 7.

For Ticket G: what does the Guide-#10 pre-inspection show about the 13 XMLs? What are the two possible EA-16 dispositions (all absolute vs. relative found)? What file is updated in either case, and where does that file live?

For Ticket I: what is the exact fix (one line, in which file)? State the final disposition that must be documented and where. For Ticket FF: what does EA-16 search for, and what are the two possible dispositions? What is updated in each case?

For Ticket XX: what did Guide-#10 find, and what is EA-16's task? What is the only permitted change if drift is found? For item H (history doc): state the file path, the 4-point content outline, and why devplatform needs this doc when BlarAI already has STATUS.md.

Describe the complete_task restoration: - What changed between Phase 6 and Phase 7? - What is Check 0f and what does EA-16 do if it PASSES vs. FAILS? - For the 4 Vikunja ticket closes (G, I, FF, XX) in Phase 7, which tool does EA-16 use if Check 0f passes?

State the commit plan: branch name, how many commits (C1 etc.), which files in each, which repo. State the backlog math: starting point, Phase 7 delta, final count after Phase 7. State what happens to remaining open tickets after Stage 6.7.5 is declared complete.

Confirm all 8 binding acks (g10-ea16_n1 through n8) are understood. For n3: name the 4 frozen verdict files. For n4: confirm the complete_task status and the Check 0f prerequisite. For n6: confirm what EA-16 must do BEFORE documenting either G or FF findings.

preflight.check: Read C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\state.json. Confirm fleet_paused=false. If fleet_paused=true: HARD STOP.

BlarAI: git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI" tag --list EXPECT: pre-platform-extract AND post-platform-extract present. devplatform: git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform" tag --list EXPECT: platform-extracted present (at ae75507). If any required tag absent: HARD STOP.

Run: python -c " from pathlib import Path gi = Path(r'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.gitignore').read_text(encoding='utf-8') print('.mcp.json present:', '.mcp.json' in gi) " EXPECT: .mcp.json present: False If True: .mcp.json already gitignored — document and skip the Ticket I gitignore edit. Note in close report either way.

check.label: Independent XML scan — python.exe path audit (Ticket G baseline)

preflight.check: Run from devplatform: python -c " from pathlib import Path xml_dir = Path(r'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\scheduled-tasks') results = {} for xml in sorted(xml_dir.glob('*.xml')): text = xml.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace') if 'python.exe' in text.lower(): has_abs = r'.venv\Scripts\python.exe' in text or r'.venv/Scripts/python.exe' in text results[xml.name] = 'ABSOLUTE' if has_abs else 'RELATIVE_OR_UNKNOWN' else: results[xml.name] = 'NO_PYTHON_EXE' for name, status in results.items(): print(f'{status:25} {name}') any_relative = any(v == 'RELATIVE_OR_UNKNOWN' for v in results.values()) print() print('Any relative/unknown python paths:', any_relative) " EXPECT: all python.exe-invoking XMLs show ABSOLUTE; no RELATIVE_OR_UNKNOWN. If any RELATIVE_OR_UNKNOWN found: fix those XMLs in C1 AND document. If all ABSOLUTE (expected): document only (no XML edits needed).

Search for any wrapper script that might wrap tools.vikunja_mcp.cli: python -c " from pathlib import Path devp = Path(r'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform') candidates = [] for ext in ['*.sh', '*.bat', '*.ps1', '*.cmd']: for f in devp.rglob(ext): if 'venv' in str(f) or '__pycache__' in str(f) or '.mypy_cache' in str(f): continue text = f.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore') if 'vikunja_mcp' in text.lower() and 'cli' in text.lower(): candidates.append(str(f)) for c in candidates: print('FOUND:', c) if not candidates: print('No wrapper scripts found.') " ALSO check vikunja_mcp directory specifically: python -c " from pathlib import Path vp = Path(r'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\vikunja_mcp') for f in vp.iterdir(): print(f.name, f.suffix) " If wrapper found: inspect it; remove if superseded by direct module invocation. If no wrapper found (expected): document in README.md.

check.label: Confirm complete_task MCP tool is RESTORED (post-Phase-6 restart)

preflight.check: Verify the running MCP server uses the fixed server.py by calling get_task on a known live task, then inspecting that complete_task source contains _api_get: python -c " import inspect, sys sys.path.insert(0, r'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform') from tools.vikunja_mcp import server src = inspect.getsource(server.complete_task) has_get = '_api_get' in src has_desc = 'description' in src print('complete_task has _api_get:', has_get) print('complete_task has description:', has_desc) print('RESTORED:', has_get and has_desc) " EXPECT: RESTORED: True If RESTORED: False — the MCP server is still running old code. Do NOT call mcp__vikunja__complete_task. Use Option B (get_task → update_task(done=True, all fields) → add_task_comment) for all Vikunja closes in Phase 7 and note in close report. If RESTORED: True — use mcp__vikunja__complete_task directly for ticket closes.
preflight.fleet_pause:
Pause the fleet using the Python helper: python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.pause_fleet('Stage 6 Phase 7 close-out: Tickets G+I+FF+XX + history doc', updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')"
Commit immediately in devplatform (on main, before branching): git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform add tools/autonomy_budget/state.json git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform commit -m "chore(ops): pause fleet -- Stage 6 Phase 7 close-out"
preflight.tracking_task: Create Phase 7 tracking task in Vikunja Project 10: Title: "Stage 6.7.5 Phase 7 tracking: close-out (Tickets G+I+FF+XX + history doc)" Priority: 2 Labels: Active (id 1), Documentation (id 7), Infrastructure (id 5) Add starting comment: "[agent:ea_code][phase:start] Phase 7 execution started. Final Stage 6.7.5 phase. Scope: G(315) I(283) FF(296) XX(328) + devplatform history doc. Pre-flight checks passed. complete_task: <RESTORED / OPTION-B> per Check 0f." Record tracking task ID for PF1 bifurcation.

execution.task:
Read devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/blarai_next_task_resolver.py in full. Verify: 1. Constructor default uses DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT from _project_context (line ~102). 2. No docstring references docs/active_tasks.yaml ambiguously. 3. continuation_template resolved against self._repo_root (BlarAI root) — correct.
If all aligned: no file changes for XX. Document "alignment confirmed" in close report. If any docstring says "repo root" ambiguously: change that line only to be explicit (e.g., "BlarAI repo root" or reference DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT). Cosmetic only.

Based on Check 0d results, add a new section to devplatform/docs/governance/fleet-hygiene.md documenting the python.exe absolute-path doctrine. Suggested placement: after the existing §4 fleet-pause section or at a logical governance section end.
Section content (adapt title and numbering to fit existing doc):
## §N — Scheduled-Task python.exe Absolute-Path Doctrine
All scheduled-task XML files that invoke Python must reference the devplatform venv python.exe by absolute path:
C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.venv\Scripts\python.exe
PATH-relative `python` or `python.exe` invocations are forbidden in XML `<Command>` and `<Arguments>` elements. Rationale: Task Scheduler runs in S4U (non-interactive) context where PATH differs from the user's interactive session; PATH-relative lookups are fragile across venv reinstalls and user profile changes.
XMLs that invoke PowerShell scripts (via wscript.exe + powershell.exe) are exempt — those scripts manage their own Python invocations internally and are covered by the direct env-var credential pattern (Phase 4, Ticket II).
Verification: run `tools/scheduled-tasks/verify_xml_python_paths.py` (see below) or the Check 0d one-liner from Phase 7 to confirm 0 relative-path invocations.
(Include Check 0d result table in the section as the baseline audit record.)
If any relative paths were found in Check 0d and fixed: reference the fix commit in the section as "corrected at Phase 7 close-out."

Edit devplatform/.gitignore. Add the following entry in the "Secrets files" section (after the .env.local line):
# MCP config (contains env-var template for VIKUNJA_PASS — not a secret, # but gitignored to prevent credential-literal drift; managed via env vars) .mcp.json
Disposition note to add to fleet-hygiene.md (same edit session as G-1, or as a sub-section of the credential pattern section):
Both .mcp.json files (BlarAI and devplatform) use ${env:VIKUNJA_PASS} template format and are gitignored. PS1 fleet scripts read credentials directly from $env:VIKUNJA_URL, $env:VIKUNJA_USER, $env:VIKUNJA_PASS (Phase 4 Ticket II). MCP loaders resolve the template at session start from the process environment. Do not commit .mcp.json files with literal passwords — if a literal credential appears in .mcp.json, rotate it.

task.label: Ticket FF — wrapper disposition + README canonical invocation

execution.task:
Based on Check 0e results:
IF wrapper found: remove it. Commit the removal in C1. Document in close report.
IF wrapper not found (expected): Update devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/README.md. Add or update the invocation section to document the canonical Option α pattern:
## Canonical Invocation (Option α)
The Vikunja MCP CLI is invoked via direct Python module execution:
python -m tools.vikunja_mcp.cli <command> [args]
Example (list projects):
cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform python -m tools.vikunja_mcp.cli list-projects
A wrapper script was considered at Stage 4 Item 4.11a but is absent (superseded by direct module invocation, which is simpler and more robust). Direct invocation is the supported pattern going forward.

Create devplatform/docs/PLATFORM_SEPARATION_HISTORY.md with the following structure (adapt wording naturally):
# Platform Separation History
## Summary
The BlarAI fleet tools were extracted from the BlarAI repository into this devplatform repository during Stage 4 of the BlarAI Platform Separation v2 procedure (cutover: 2026-04-28, BlarAI HEAD at cutover: 8f60259).
Full documentation — narrative, decision log, per-phase records, and anomaly register — lives at:
BlarAI/docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md
Stage 6.7.5 hardening (7 execution phases, 2026-05-07 to 2026-05-08) completed the post-extraction cleanup. See STATUS.md entries E8–E15.
## Key Architectural Decisions
These decisions explain why the devplatform code is shaped the way it is. Each entry gives the rationale; the full record is in STATUS.md.
**Env-var credential pattern** (Phase 4, Ticket II — devplatform commit d478c05): Five PS1 fleet scripts were refactored away from reading .mcp.json via ConvertFrom-Json. They now read $env:VIKUNJA_URL, $env:VIKUNJA_USER, $env:VIKUNJA_PASS directly. Rationale: .mcp.json VIKUNJA_PASS was moved to template format (${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}), breaking the ConvertFrom-Json read path. Direct env-var reads are simpler and consistent with Python cli.py.
**Cross-repo path constant** (Phase 5, Ticket VV — devplatform commit bebd6e2): DEFAULT_ROSTER_PATH in tools/autonomy_budget/active_tasks.py, and the repo_root defaults in proactive_colead.py and proactive_sdo.py, now derive from DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT (tools/_project_context.py) rather than Path(__file__).parents[2] (which resolves to the devplatform root, not BlarAI). The active_tasks.yaml roster lives in BlarAI — devplatform cannot resolve it via self-relative paths.
**Absolute python.exe paths in scheduled-task XMLs** (Phase 7, Ticket G): All scheduled-task XML files that invoke Python use the devplatform venv absolute path: C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.venv\Scripts\python.exe. PATH-relative invocations are prohibited (Task Scheduler S4U context differs from interactive PATH). See fleet-hygiene.md §N.
**.mcp.json template format + gitignored** (Phase 7, Ticket I): Both BlarAI/.mcp.json and devplatform/.mcp.json use ${env:VIKUNJA_PASS} template format and are gitignored. Credential values live in User-scope Windows environment variables set at Stage 0 provisioning. Do not commit literal passwords to .mcp.json.
**Direct module invocation for Vikunja MCP CLI** (Phase 7, Ticket FF): python -m tools.vikunja_mcp.cli is the canonical invocation. No wrapper script exists; direct invocation (Option α, Stage 4 Item 4.11a) is the supported pattern.
**complete_task data-loss fix** (Phase 6, Ticket WW — devplatform commit ea2fbf5): mcp__vikunja__complete_task previously sent only {"done": True} to the Vikunja POST /tasks/{id} endpoint, causing Go zero-value overwriting of description and priority. The fix reads the current task first (GET), then POSTs with all fields preserved plus done=True. See tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py.
## Related Repositories
- BlarAI: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI (application code, docs, use cases) - devplatform: C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform (fleet tools, scheduled tasks, MCP server)
execution.commit:
Stage exactly the changed files (explicit-add). Expected set: git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform add .gitignore git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform add docs/governance/fleet-hygiene.md git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform add tools/vikunja_mcp/README.md git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform add docs/PLATFORM_SEPARATION_HISTORY.md
If XX had cosmetic changes: git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform add tools/autonomy_budget/blarai_next_task_resolver.py
If G found and fixed relative XML paths: add those XMLs too. If FF found and removed a wrapper: add the deletion too (git rm wrapper_file).
Commit message: "docs(stage6_phase7): Tickets G+I+FF+XX close-out + devplatform history doc"
Do NOT use git add . or git add -A.
execution.vikunja_closes:
Use mcp__vikunja__complete_task if Check 0f confirmed RESTORED. Fall back to Option B (get_task → update_task(done=True, fields) → add_task_comment) if Check 0f showed the old code is still running.
Close in order (add a comment to each):
Ticket G (315): "[agent:ea_code][phase:completion] Ticket G complete. All 13 scheduled-task XMLs scanned. <N> invoke python.exe — all use absolute venv path. 7 invoke wscript/powershell (no python.exe). <No fix required / N XMLs fixed.> fleet-hygiene.md updated with python.exe absolute-path doctrine. Phase 7 commit: <C1 SHA>."
Ticket I (283): "[agent:ea_code][phase:completion] Ticket I complete. devplatform/.gitignore updated with .mcp.json entry. Both .mcp.json files confirmed template format. Final disposition documented in fleet-hygiene.md. Phase 7 commit: <C1 SHA>."
Ticket FF (296): "[agent:ea_code][phase:completion] Ticket FF complete. n1(l) wrapper search: <not found / found and removed>. vikunja_mcp/README.md updated with canonical Option α invocation. Phase 7 commit: <C1 SHA>."
Ticket XX (328): "[agent:ea_code][phase:completion] Ticket XX complete. blarai_next_task_resolver.py read in full. <Alignment confirmed — no changes / cosmetic fix applied: describe>. Phase 7 commit: <C1 SHA>."

edit.instruction:
Append evolution entry E15 and the Stage 6.7.5 closure marker:
E15: "Stage 6 Phase 7 (FINAL) complete. Tickets G (315): all 13 scheduled-task XMLs confirmed using absolute python.exe venv paths; doctrine documented in fleet-hygiene.md. Ticket I (283): devplatform/.gitignore updated with .mcp.json entry; template+gitignored disposition recorded. Ticket FF (296): n1(l) wrapper <not found / removed>; direct module invocation (Option α) documented in README. Ticket XX (328): blarai_next_task_resolver.py alignment <confirmed / fixed>. devplatform/docs/PLATFORM_SEPARATION_HISTORY.md created (pointer + key decisions). Stage 6.7.5 hardening DECLARED COMPLETE. Remaining 15 open tickets migrate to Phase 5 post-operational backlog. Backlog: 44 total / 29 done / 15 open."
Commit in BlarAI on main: git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI add docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI commit -m "docs(stage6_phase7): Phase 7 complete + Stage 6.7.5 declared closed (E15)"

execution.merge: git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform checkout main git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform merge --no-ff feat/stage6-phase7-close-out -m "docs(stage6_phase7): Stage 6 Phase 7 -- close-out Tickets G+I+FF+XX + history doc"
execution.fleet_unpause:
python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.resume_fleet(updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')"
git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform add tools/autonomy_budget/state.json git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform commit -m "chore(ops): unpause fleet -- Stage 6 Phase 7 complete; Stage 6.7.5 CLOSED"
execution.pf1_bifurcation: PASS path: close tracking task via complete_task (if restored) or Option B. HARD STOP path: comment with failure summary, leave done=false. Per AOAM §4.

ea_dispatch.close_report_format:
Write close report to: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\platform_separation\temp_for_responses\6.7.5-EA_INSTANCE_16_STAGE6_PHASE_7_CLOSE_REPORT.md
Report must cover: 1. Phase 7 PASS / HARD STOP verdict 2. Pre-flight results (0a/0b/0c/0d/0e/0f) 3. Each work item result: G (scan table + doc), I (gitignore + disposition), FF (search result + README), XX (alignment finding), H (history doc created) 4. Commit SHAs: pause, C1, STATUS.md, merge, unpause (both repos) 5. Vikunja: G/I/FF/XX/tracking all done=True; which tool used (complete_task or Option B) 6. Stage 6.7.5 closure statement 7. Backlog: total / done / open; remaining tickets deferred to Phase 5 backlog
Post short summary (5-10 lines) to chat.

constraints.constraint: Four frozen verdict files must NOT be edited: docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.0.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_AUDIT_CHECKPOINT.xml docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.1.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_ITEM_3_1_V2_VERIFIED_SPEC_CORRECTION_AUTHORIZED.xml docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.2.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_ITEM_3_2_V2_RECLASSIFICATION_REQUIRED_SELECTIVE_COPY_AUTHORIZED.xml docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.10-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_CLOSE_VERIFIED.xml
All git add commands name files explicitly. Never git add . or git add -A.

EA-16 must run all pre-flight checks (0c, 0d, 0e, 0f) independently before treating Guide-#10 findings as fact. Only the Check results observed by EA-16 determine disposition. If EA-16 finds a discrepancy with Guide-#10 findings: report it in the close report; do not silently accept the Guide finding.

Phase 7 closes exactly G(315), I(283), FF(296), XX(328) plus creates H. No other open Stage 6.7.5 tickets are in scope. Do not expand scope. If additional defects are found during work: document in close report and note as "deferred to Phase 5 post-operational backlog."
Ticket FF disposition is documentation only (remove wrapper if found, else document its absence). Ticket XX is read-verify (cosmetic fix only if found). Neither ticket warrants any logic changes.

exclusions.item: All other 15 open Stage 6.7.5 tickets — deferred to Phase 5 post-operational backlog.
Any BlarAI code changes — Phase 7 touches only STATUS.md in BlarAI.
fleet-status.ps1 (Ticket RR 307), wake template automation (Ticket GG 297), XML re-registration (Ticket HH 298) — all deferred.

acknowledgements.ack: Explicit-add discipline: all git add commands name files explicitly. Never git add . or git add -A.
No-amend doctrine: 4 frozen verdict files (3.0.v2, 3.1.v2, 3.2.v2, 3.10) must not be edited. These are in docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/.

complete_task RESTORED (Phase 6 fix merged; user restarted Claude Code). EA-16 MUST verify via Check 0f before calling mcp__vikunja__complete_task. If Check 0f PASS: use complete_task directly for all 4 ticket closes + tracking. If Check 0f FAIL (old code still running): use Option B for all closes; document.

Two-repo tag check (Check 0b): BlarAI must have pre-platform-extract + post-platform-extract. devplatform must have platform-extracted at ae75507. platform-extracted absent from BlarAI is not a defect.

Verify-first discipline: EA-16 must independently run Check 0d (XML scan) before documenting Ticket G disposition, and Check 0e (wrapper search) before documenting Ticket FF disposition. Guide-#10 findings are context, not gospel. Report any discrepancy in the close report.

Stage 6.7.5 closure: Phase 7 is the FINAL execution phase. After Phase 7 merges, STATUS.md E15 declares Stage 6.7.5 complete. The 15 remaining open tickets in Vikunja Project 10 are NOT closed — they migrate to the Phase 5 post-operational backlog and will be addressed in future sprints. Do not close or modify those tickets.
PF1 bifurcation per AOAM §4: PASS path closes tracking task (done=True); HARD STOP path adds comment + leaves done=false.

guide_verification.check: devplatform: git log --oneline shows unpause → merge → C1 → pause
devplatform: git show C1_SHA --stat shows expected files changed
devplatform: docs/governance/fleet-hygiene.md has python.exe doctrine section
devplatform: tools/vikunja_mcp/README.md has Option α invocation
BlarAI: STATUS.md E15 entry present; Stage 6.7.5 declared closed
Vikunja: G(315), I(283), FF(296), XX(328) all done=true
guide_verification.backlog_expectation: After Phase 6: 43 total / 24 done / 19 open Phase 7: +1 total (tracking task) / +5 done (G+I+FF+XX+tracking) / -4 open (G+I+FF+XX) After Phase 7: 44 total / 29 done / 15 open
